tracks.seed: deep protein-format design review: for EGFR colorectal cancer, what protein is best for a conjugate? compare IgG with kappa/lambda light chain considerations, VHH/nanobody, scFv, Fab, Fc, minibody, SIP, affibody, adnectin, anticalin, DARPin, knottin, cyclic peptide, bispecific/trispecific/multispecific formats, chemistry, DAR, PK/PD, affinity and avidity.
forbiddenTerms: one format is proven without data

followUps.prompt: when would full IgG beat VHH, scFv, Fab, minibody, or SIP for this EGFR case?
when would a bispecific or multispecific format beat a monospecific IgG?
compare IgG-scFv, IgG-dAb, scFv-Fc-scFv, KiH-IgG, kappa-lambda body, and tandem scFv for target gating
what DAR or loading range would change if the protein format is smaller than IgG?
which chemistry could hurt binding, internalization, Fc behavior, or avidity?
how should affinity be tuned so we do not create a binding-site barrier or normal tissue trap?
what are the advantages and disadvantages of affibody, adnectin, anticalin, DARPin, knottin, and abdurin-like scaffolds?
could a cyclic peptide be better than a protein scaffold here?
what modifications like Fc engineering, albumin binding, PEG, masking, or charge tuning are justified only if required?
if EGFR is sparsely expressed in tumor cells, how does that change protein format, affinity, avidity, and DAR?
if EGFR is heavily expressed in normal epithelial tissue and we still want a cytotoxic payload, how does carrier PK/PD dominate the design?
if the antigen is shed or soluble, what happens to IgG versus compact scaffolds?
if the target-bearing cancer cell recycles EGFR instead of degrading it, how does that alter IgG versus Fab/VHH and linker choice?
how would hypoxia, acidic pH, proteases, stroma, and interstitial pressure change protein selection?
give the final protein-format selection rule without hallucinating validation

tracks.seed: deep protein-format design review: for Parkinson disease conjugates, what protein or scaffold is best to deliver a non-cytotoxic active species into CNS tissue? compare VHH/nanobody, scFv, Fab, Fc, minibody, SIP, affibody, DARPin, cyclic peptide, IgG, bispecific shuttle-plus-target formats, affinity, avidity, PK/PD, chemistry, and safety.

followUps.prompt: why might full IgG be worse than VHH, scFv, Fab, or cyclic peptide for CNS delivery?
what bispecific format could combine BBB shuttle and disease target without overclaiming?
how do affinity and avidity affect brain endothelial binding, transcytosis, and peripheral sink?
which chemistry could block shuttle function or endosomal escape?
how would PK/PD separate plasma, CSF, brain region, neuron, glia, and active compartment exposure?
are affibodies, DARPins, anticalins, knottins, or abdurins credible here?
when would albumin binding or Fc fusion help versus hurt CNS delivery?
what safety risks come from chronic CNS transport handles?
give a step-by-step protein-format screen before payload chemistry optimization
if the CNS entry antigen is abundant on brain endothelium but also peripheral tissue, how does location change PK/PD and format choice?
what if the transport antigen is internalized fast but mostly recycles instead of transcytosing?
if brain endothelial cells internalize the shuttle but degrade it instead of transcytosing, what protein format changes?
how should neuron versus glia versus endothelial antigen-bearing cells change protein selection?
give the most honest provisional protein recommendation

tracks.seed: deep protein-format design review: for myasthenia gravis, choose protein formats for FcRn/IgG-lowering, complement modulation, autoantigen-specific tolerance, or tissue protection. compare IgG, Fc, Fab, scFv, VHH, minibody, SIP, bispecific/trispecific formats, cyclic peptides, enzymes if relevant, affinity, avidity, PK/PD, chemistry, and chronic safety.

followUps.prompt: which protein format best fits FcRn or IgG-lowering without broad cytotoxic depletion?
when would a bispecific help: FcRn plus autoantigen, complement plus tissue handle, or immune-cell targeting?
what affinity or avidity would avoid over-suppressing normal IgG biology?
could cyclic peptides or compact scaffolds beat IgG here?
when are enzymes relevant, and when should enzyme formats stay out?
which chemistry would preserve receptor binding and avoid immunogenicity?
how would repeat dosing and chronic PK/PD change protein-format ranking?
what modifications are justified only if required?
make a go/no-go checklist for protein format selection
if FcRn is everywhere in recycling compartments, how does antigen location change the protein and PK/PD design?
if complement biology is extracellular and not internalizing, what protein format logic changes?
how does immune-cell activation state or extracellular complement microenvironment change internalization assumptions?
if target-bearing immune cells recycle the receptor instead of routing to lysosome, how does chemistry and payload choice change?
give the safest provisional format direction

tracks.seed: deep protein-format design review: VWSR1 is an unvalidated hypothetical surface target in a hypothetical disease. choose between IgG, VHH/nanobody, scFv, Fab, Fc, minibody, SIP, affibody, adnectin, anticalin, DARPin, knottin, cyclic peptide, bispecific/trispecific/multispecific formats, enzymes only if relevant, affinity, avidity, chemistry, DAR/loading, PK/PD, and safety without hallucinating validation.

followUps.prompt: what should we validate before picking IgG, nanobody, scFv, Fab, or engineered scaffold?
if VWSR1 does not internalize, which protein formats remain plausible?
how should affinity and avidity be tuned when antigen density is unknown?
what DAR/loading should we avoid before binding-after-conjugation is known?
which chemistry should be screened to avoid blocking the binding surface?
could a bispecific or trispecific be justified without disease validation?
what modifications should not be added until a real barrier is measured?
give advantages and disadvantages of compact scaffolds without pretending VWSR1 binders exist
make a stepwise protein-format decision cascade
if VWSR1 is sparsely expressed and also present in normal marrow, how does that change protein format and payload risk?
if VWSR1 is surface accessible but shed into blood, what happens to carrier PK/PD?
if VWSR1 internalization differs across cell types, how do we avoid hallucinating one trafficking route?
if the microenvironment is hypoxic and protease-rich but unvalidated, how should protein selection stay conditional?
give the safest no-hallucination protein recommendation
